From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Sunday Telegraph](/w/index.php?title=Sunday_Telegraph&redirect=no "Sunday Telegraph"))

British broadsheet newspaper

This article is about the UK newspaper. For the Australian "Sunday Telegraph" newspaper, see  [*The Sunday Telegraph* (Sydney)](/wiki/The_Sunday_Telegraph_(Sydney) "The Sunday Telegraph (Sydney)"). For the American "Sunday Telegraph" newspaper in Nashua, New Hampshire, see  [*The Telegraph* (Nashua, New Hampshire)](/wiki/The_Telegraph_(Nashua,_New_Hampshire) "The Telegraph (Nashua, New Hampshire)").

The Sunday Telegraph

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Type | [Weekly newspaper](/wiki/Weekly_newspaper "Weekly newspaper") |
| Format | [Broadsheet](/wiki/Broadsheet "Broadsheet") |
| Owner | [Telegraph Media Group](/wiki/Telegraph_Media_Group "Telegraph Media Group") |
| Editor | [Allister Heath](/wiki/Allister_Heath "Allister Heath") |
| Founded | 5 February 1961; 65 years ago (1961-02-05) |
| Political alignment | [Conservative](/wiki/Conservative_Party_(UK) "Conservative Party (UK)")[[1]](#cite_note-1) |
| Headquarters | 111 Buckingham Palace Road, London, SW1W 0DT |
| [Circulation](/wiki/Newspaper_circulation "Newspaper circulation") | 248,288 (as of December 2019)[[2]](#cite_note-2) |
| Sister newspapers | *[The Daily Telegraph](/wiki/The_Daily_Telegraph "The Daily Telegraph")* |
| [ISSN](/wiki/ISSN_(identifier) "ISSN (identifier)") | [0307-269X](https://www.worldcat.org/search?fq=x0:jrnl&q=n2:0307-269X) |
| [OCLC](/wiki/OCLC_(identifier) "OCLC (identifier)") number | [436617202](https://www.worldcat.org/oclc/436617202) |
| Website | [telegraph.co.uk](https://telegraph.co.uk) |

***The Sunday Telegraph*** is a British [broadsheet newspaper](/wiki/Broadsheet_newspaper "Broadsheet newspaper"), first published on 5 February 1961 by the [Telegraph Media Group](/wiki/Telegraph_Media_Group "Telegraph Media Group"). It is the [sister paper](/wiki/Sister_paper "Sister paper") of *[The Daily Telegraph](/wiki/The_Daily_Telegraph "The Daily Telegraph")*, also published by the Telegraph Media Group. *The Sunday Telegraph* was originally a separate operation with different editorial staff, but since 2013 the *Telegraph* has been a seven-day operation.[[3]](#cite_note-3) However, *The Sunday Telegraph* still has its own editor, different from that of *The Daily Telegraph*.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

When the Sunday Telegraph was launched in February 1961, by [William Micheal Berry](/wiki/William_Michael_Berry,_3rd_Viscount_Camrose "William Michael Berry, 3rd Viscount Camrose"), it was the first new national Sunday newspaper to launch for over 40 years. Another member of the Berry family owned the [Sunday Times](/wiki/The_Sunday_Times "The Sunday Times") and there had been an agreement that no Sunday edition of The Telegraph would be created. When [Viscount Kemsley](/wiki/Viscount_Kemsley "Viscount Kemsley") sold the Sunday Times in 1959, William Berry decided to launch the paper. The newspaper distinguished itself from other titles by containing more news than was typical.[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)

In 1994 they became one of the first traditional UK newspapers to launch online when the Electronic Telegraph website went live. The Electronic Telegraph was part of a brand repositioning strategy for The Telegraph and The Sunday Telegraph, to help the titles appeal to younger affluent readers.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

The Sunday Telegraph operated independently from The Telegraph until 2013, when the newspapers became a single operation, the result of changes made due to commercial pressures, the impact of the internet on the newspaper industry and a reduction in advertising rates.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

Since 2020, The Telegraph and The Sunday Telegraph have chosen to keep their Audit Bureau of Circulations (ABC) readership numbers private,[[9]](#cite_note-9) with the last known figures for The Sunday Telegraph being 248,288 (December 2019).[[10]](#cite_note-10) [YouGov](/wiki/YouGov "YouGov") surveys have shown that 91% of people know about The Sunday Telegraph and 31% of people have a positive opinion about the newspaper (although it is disliked by 25% of people asked).[[11]](#cite_note-11)

In March 2026 it was announced that [Axel Springer SE](/wiki/Axel_Springer_SE "Axel Springer SE") had agreed to purchase the Telegraph Media Group for £575 million (including The Sunday Telegraph), after the groups ownership had been in limbo since 2023.[[12]](#cite_note-12)
